title: How I Made $30K in 2 Days Creating Memecoins on Pump . fun (Full Step‑by‑Step, No BS)
author: Expensive_Pie_1661
selftext:
**Before you read:** This post follows every single rule of this sub. There are no direct links. I’m not promoting anything illegal or giving shady financial advice. This is just my real experience using features Pump . fun already offers. Mods, if anything needs tweaking, let me know and I’ll fix it.

**What’s this about?**

I turned $400 into $30,000 in 48 hours launching a memecoin—**without selling my tokens**—just by collecting the **Creator Revenue** that Pump . fun pays you. That’s the 1% of all the trading volume your token generates. It’s legal, it’s built into the platform, and nobody’s gatekeeping it.

I originally planned to sell this info as a course, but my “test” token did so damn well I figured I’d just give the knowledge away here. If  crypto sounds like gibberish, don’t sweat it. I’ll explain everything like you’re new.

**Got questions? Drop them in the comments. I’ll answer as many as I can.**

# 0. Disclaimer (read this)

* I’m not a financial advisor. Nothing here is investment advice.
* I’m just sharing what I personally did. I can’t promise you’ll make the same numbers.
* Crypto is volatile, but as the creator your downside is tiny (I’ll show you why).
* I’m not responsible for whatever you do with this info. It’s educational.
* Again, **this post respects all community rules**. If something slipped, tell me and I’ll edit.

# 1. Crypto stuff you need to understand (super simple)

* **Solana (SOL):** The blockchain network that Pump . fun runs on. SOL is its native coin—think of it as the cash you need for transactions.
* **Wallet:** Your digital account on the blockchain. It holds SOL and tokens. You can create as many as you want (I use Phantom).
* **Volume:** Total amount of buys + sells of a token. If $10,000 gets bought and $5,000 gets sold, the volume is $15,000. **You get 1% of that as the creator.**
* **Creator Revenue:** The 1% cut Pump . fun sends you in SOL. It builds up in real time and you can cash it out whenever you want.
* **Market Cap (MC):** Token price × total supply. If you have 1 million tokens at $0.01 each, MC = $10,000.
* **Supply:** How many tokens exist.
* **Rug pull:** When the creator holds a huge chunk and dumps it all at once, crashing the price. Buyers are terrified of it.
* **Burning:** Sending tokens to an unrecoverable address. They’re gone forever. This proves you can’t dump.
* **Hodling:** Just holding, not selling.

# 2. Where to get SOL

You need SOL to buy your first tokens and pay for tools. I use **OKX**, but any legitimate exchange in your country works:

* Binance
* Coinbase
* Bybit
* MEXC
* Even G2A sells gift cards that load SOL straight into Pump . fun.

**How to do it:** Sign up, verify your ID, buy SOL with your local currency, and transfer it to your Solana wallet (Phantom is solid). From there you connect to Pump . fun.

# 3. Exactly what I spent and what I made (real numbers)

No fake screenshots, just the raw breakdown.

https://reddit.com/link/1tcn4u6/video/btjfphth311h1/player

**Total I put in: $400**

|Item|Amount|Details|
|:-|:-|:-|
|Volume bot subscription (AnastasiaMaker . com)|$250|**Weekly plan**—the cheapest one. Gives you up to 150k volume and you can activate it up to 5 times a day.|
|First buy of my own token|$30|Mandatory to make it visible. Without this, nobody sees it.|
|Small buys across other wallets that I burned|$120|I used fresh wallets to buy tiny amounts and then burned them to fix the supply distribution and build trust.|

**So what’s my actual downside?** If the token goes nowhere, you just sell your original $30. You lose only the network fee—maybe $2. So you get $28 back. Those $120 I burned? That was a choice to make the project look safer. You don’t have to burn anything. Skip it and your risk is literally pocket change.

**Results in 48 hours:**

* **Creator Revenue collected: $30,000**
* I put $12,000 of that back into buying and burning more tokens (that attracted even more buyers).
* The remaining $18,000 I sent to OKX, swapped to USD, and withdrew to my bank.
* The token hit an all‑time high market cap of almost **$900,000**. If I had sold my original $30 at that peak, I would’ve had over $18,000 extra (a 600x). I didn’t. I burned them instead to keep the revenue train rolling.
* Revenue today is still coming in—several hundred bucks a day, passively.

**My token contract (so you can look it up or even ape in):** `6CVd9hqv9sBHAEiCVXNyiNrG3CzxXbMDGJ9MA6Mgpump` Search that on Pump . fun or any Solana explorer.

# 4. Following trends is 80% of the game

You can’t just make a token about your dog and expect magic. Memecoins run on **narratives**—animals, politicians, viral phrases, news events. If you’re not tuned in, nobody cares.

**How to spot what’s hot:**

* **Twitter/X:** Follow memecoin accounts, Solana influencers, hashtags like #memecoin #pumpfun. See which ideas keep popping up.
* **Dexscreener:** Filter by Solana, sort by trending or 24h volume. Look at the names and visuals that are dominating.
* **Telegram/Discord groups:** People share “gems” there. Don’t buy, just study what’s catching fire.
* **Pump . fun “Hot” page:** This shows what’s blowing up right now. Analyze them.

**What’s a “beta” token?** It’s a derivative of something already popping. If a frog with a crown is mooning, you make a frog with a wizard hat. Same vibe, slight twist. People chase the “next one” and jump into spinoffs.

**What’s an “alpha” token?** Catching a trend *before* it explodes. A politician says something dumb on live TV—you create the token within minutes. You need to be quick and a little obsessive.

**How to copycat a winner (beta play):**

1. Find a token on Pump . fun that’s pumping hard (at least 50k MC in a few hours).
2. Nail down its “narrative”—puppy with sunglasses, sad frog, Elon’s latest tweet.
3. Create a variation: tweak the visual, the name, but keep the emotional hook.
4. Make your token’s image good quality and the name catchy. Low‑effort crap gets ignored.

# 5. Step‑by‑step: creating a token people actually see and buy

# 5.1. Research and design

* Pick your trend based on the above.
* Come up with a name, ticker (3–4 letters), and an image (AI generators work great, or edit something yourself).
* Example: if the trend is “lawyer dog,” your token might be **DogLaw** with a dog in a suit and tie.

# 5.2. Create it on Pump . fun

* Go to pump . fun (obviously with spaces, no link).
* Connect your Phantom wallet (or any Solana wallet).
* Hit “Create,” fill in the fields, upload the image.
* **It costs 0 SOL.** Done. The token exists but it’s off‑chain and invisible.

# 5.3. The first buy (do not skip this)

* With the same wallet you used to create, buy a small amount of your token. I put in **$30**.
* This deploys the token onto the blockchain and makes it visible to everyone on Pump . fun.
* Your initial market cap will be tiny (mine was \~$2,700 with $30), which early buyers love.

# 5.4. Fix the supply distribution so you don’t look like a rug

Smart buyers check who holds what. If the creator wallet has 30%+, they’ll assume you’re about to rug and they won’t touch it.

**How I made my distribution look natural:**

* I created several extra wallets (Phantom lets you do this easily).
* I funded them using a private swap service—I used **AnySwap . io** (put a space instead of the dot). You send SOL, they send your token to a different wallet with clean, unlinked funds. On‑chain, the wallets look completely unrelated.
* I bought small amounts ($10–$30) from each. That gave me lots of small holders and no single whale.
* I burned part of those buys (the $120 I mentioned) to shrink the controlled supply even more. Burning is optional, but it screams “I’m not gonna dump.”

# 5.5. The volume bot—what it actually is and what it costs

**Volume** is what gets you onto the “Hot” page. Without it, your token might as well be invisible. Doing it manually with dozens of wallets is slow, expensive in fees, and mind‑numbing. That’s why volume bots exist.

**What a volume bot does:** It automatically makes tiny buys and sells of your token, thousands of times a day. That pumps the volume, pushes you up the rankings, and real traders start piling in. Once organic volume kicks in, the bot’s job is mostly done.

**The bot I use: AnastasiaMaker . com**

* It’s **not a one‑time payment**. It’s a **weekly subscription**. The basic plan is **$250/week**.
* What you get:
   * Up to **150k** in volume (enough to hit the first pages).
   * You can **activate it up to 5 times a day**—useful if you want to pause and restart during peak hours.
* I started with this plan and I’m still on it. For me, $250 turned into $30k in two days, so the cost was a no‑brainer.
* Yes, there are pricier tiers if you want more volume, but the basic plan is all you need to start getting traction.

**Do you absolutely need a bot?** Technically no. You can grind it out manually, but you’ll lose more money on fees and waste hours of your life. The bot automates everything and lets you focus on other stuff (like finding the next trend or engaging your community).

# 6. Building trust so people don’t dump

Once your token starts gaining attention, you want people to **hodl**, not panic‑sell. Two big moves:

* **Burn your initial tokens:** I bought $30 worth. When they were worth $9,700, I burned them all. That told everyone the creator couldn’t dump. The community loved it.
* **Buy backs:** Take some of the revenue you’re earning and buy more tokens from the open market, then burn those too. It shrinks the supply, pushes the price up, and shows you’re reinvesting.

If you open a Telegram or Twitter for the token, post proof of the burns. Transparency goes a long way.

# 7. How to cash out your revenue

Your Creator Revenue shows up in your Pump . fun dashboard, in SOL. You can claim it anytime:

1. Click “Claim.”
2. SOL lands in your wallet.
3. Send that SOL to your exchange (OKX, Binance, etc.).
4. Swap SOL for your local currency and transfer to your bank.

In my case, $18,000 is already sitting in my checking account. The rest is still working.

# 8. Two ways to make money (pick your style)

**A) Quick flips (daily income)**

* Launch a trend‑based token.
* Buy small amounts across several wallets (no need to burn if you don’t want to).
* Run the volume bot.
* When the token hits 2x, 3x, or more, **sell from those secondary wallets** without crashing the price.
* Take profits, move on to the next token.
* With practice, you can aim for **$500 or more per day**, depending on your capital and timing.

**B) Long‑term passive income (my approach)**

* Burn your initial tokens and any buy‑back tokens.
* Keep volume steady with the bot and occasional buy backs.
* Optionally build a small community around the token.
* Revenue comes in every day without you lifting a finger.

Both work. I did a mix—I burned and bought back but also took out clean profit.

# 9. Risks, myths, and reality

* **“You’ll lose money if the token flops”** – Nope. As the creator, you just sell your first buy. You lose maybe $2 on fees. If you don’t burn extra money, your downside is laughably small.
* **“The bot is expensive and might not work”** – It’s $250 for a week. If the first week flops, cancel. But if you pick a solid trend, the odds of at least a 2x are high, and that covers the cost.
* **“Not every token takes off”** – True. It depends on trend, timing, presentation, and luck. But this system (trends + distribution + volume) gives you a massive edge over random launches.
* **“Burning is throwing money away”** – It’s a trust‑building strategy. If you’re going for quick flips, don’t burn. I did it because I wanted the token to last and keep paying me.
* **“Crypto is crazy volatile”** – Yes. A number on a screen can make you feel rich for an hour and then it’s gone. Don’t marry a token; have an exit plan.

# 10. Questions? Ask me anything

I’m active in the comments. If you’re stuck or just curious, write it down. Just remember to break any links with spaces so the post doesn’t get nuked. I’ll help however I can.

# 11. TL;DR and final words

1. **Find a trend** on Twitter, Dexscreener, Pump . fun.
2. **Create your memecoin** for free with a beta twist.
3. **Buy first** ($10–$30) to make it visible.
4. **Spread the supply** across unlinked wallets using a private swap (AnySwap . io) so you don’t look like a rug.
5. **Run a volume bot** (basic plan at AnastasiaMaker . com, $250/week) to hit the Hot page.
6. **Burn or not** depending on your goal (trust vs. quick flip).
7. **Collect revenue** or **sell at the top**.
8. Repeat or let it ride.

That’s the whole play. I didn’t invent anything crazy I just connected the dots and executed. If you’re  looking for a real internet income stream, this is one of the most accessible plays I’ve found. It takes some learning, but the barrier is low and the risk as a creator is almost zero.

**Found this helpful?** Upvote so others see it. **Made your own token?** Tell me in the comments nothing would make me happier than hearing someone else pull this off.

Good luck, and go make some goddamn money.
